breakdown: Naming (15%): 95 - Code already well-named; change from timezone.utc to UTC is a standard Python convention. Error Handling (20%): 100 - No error handling changes; this is a cosmetic modernization. Type Safety (20%): 95 - Uses proper timezone objects (UTC from datetime.timezone), maintains type safety. Architecture (20%): 95 - Single file change, no module boundaries affected, no circular imports. Test Coverage (15%): 90 - This is a test infrastructure file; changes are backward-compatible and don't require new tests. Documentation (10%): 80 - No docstrings added for the change, though code is self-explanatory.
key_changes: Replace deprecated timezone.utc with UTC constant from datetime module (Python 3.11+ best practice)
Update datetime.now(timezone.utc) to datetime.now(UTC) across 10+ locations
Update datetime.fromtimestamp(..., tz=timezone.utc) to datetime.fromtimestamp(..., tz=UTC)
Change import from 'from datetime import date, datetime, timezone' to 'from datetime import UTC, date, datetime'
